status_detail: Europe's largest operational green hydrogen plant at Yara's Porsgrunn site, Norway. 24 MW alkaline electrolysis developed and built by Linde Engineering. Inaugurated June 2024. Produces 10 tonnes/day of green hydrogen for ammonia fertilizer production. Replaces fossil-based hydrogen at Yara's ammonia plant.

data_management.narrative_summary: Yara Porsgrunn is a 24 MW alkaline electrolysis plant at Yara's fertilizer production site in Porsgrunn, Norway. Inaugurated in June 2024, it was Europe's largest operational green hydrogen facility at the time of commissioning. Developed and built by Linde Engineering, the plant produces up to 10 tonnes of green hydrogen per day, which replaces fossil-based hydrogen in Yara's ammonia production, reducing CO2 emissions by up to 41,000 tonnes per year. The project leverages Norway's abundant hydroelectric power and demonstrates large-scale alkaline electrolysis for ammonia decarbonization.

power.grid_connection_detail: Grid-connected at Porsgrunn industrial site. Norwegian hydroelectric grid >98% renewable.

sources.title: Yara inaugurates 24 MW green hydrogen plant at Porsgrunn — June 2024
sources.author: Yara International / Linde Engineering
